descript.abstract: The purpose of this dataset is to display the extent of existing Terrestrial Ecological Unit inventory (TEUI) data internally to facilitate inter-agency collaboration. The feature class for this dataset will display polygons of the ecological unit plots, acreages, and percent coverages of National Forest and Grassland administrative boundaries using their common names, with a percent coverage for Land Type and acres of forest per plot.
descript.purpose: This feature dataset was developed with the intention of keeping TEUI program managers, natural resource officers, and other forest-level supervisors updated on the status of various TEUI ecological units currently extant in the EDW on a national, regional, and individual forest scale.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

dataqual.logic: In some cases, private inholdings within National Forest boundaries may be included in zonal statistics.

horizpa.horizpar: The spatial accuracy of this dataset has not been assessed.

procstep.procdesc: TEUI data collection is a systematic inventory and mapping process that begins with climatic gradient analysis and iterative polygon delineation; it involves multiple stages of field documentation and validation. Models are generated and improved resulting in the creation of Ecological Map Units with individual descriptions of unit composition, distribution and extent. The FME process involves overlaying the TEUI LT/LTA/LTP on Forest Boundaries.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The type of national forest system unit.
attr.attrdefs: Common Names Decision table maintained by the FS office of communication

edom.edomvd: The type of national forest system unit.
Area is in a National Grassland.
Area is in a National Grassland, but the boundaries are simplified for use in mapping at smaller scales.

attr.attrdefs: Land Status Records System Administrative Forest Boundaries
Common Names Decision table maintained by the FS office of communication

attr.attrdef: Percent LTA coverage. Landtype Associations (LTA) depict broad repeating patterns of landforms, soil families or subgroups, and potential natural vegetation (PNV) series at the landscape scale. Private inholdings may be included in Zonal Stats
Area of a feature in acres. Private inholdings may be included in Zonal Stats.
Percent LT coverage. Landtypes (LT) represent finer scale, land-unit level of ecological units and are aggregated to form LTAs. Private inholdings may be included in Zonal Stats

Percent LTP coverage. Landtype Phases (LTP) constitute the finest and most detailed scale of ecological units in the National Hierarchy. They are used for comprehensive forest/prairie plan revisions, watershed assessments, burned area emergency rehabilitation efforts, wildlife habitat analysis, and project level implementation and analysis. Private inholdings may be included in Zonal Stats

The Common Name ID is a unique identifier assigned to each forest so it can be used in generating mobile map packages for the Interactive Visitor Map Mobile Offline version available for download. The forest organization code used by the administrative forest boundaries is not unique for every forest common name since some forests can have the same shared admin boundary.
attr.attrdefs: Common Names Decision table maintained by the FS office of communication

attr.attrdef: The common names of the forest and grassland as per the Common Names Decision table maintained by the FS office of communication
attr.attrdefs: Land Status Records System Administrative Forest Boundaries, Proclaimed Forest Boundaries, National Grassland Areas
Land Status Records System Administrative Forest Boundaries

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

distinfo.distliab: The U.S. Forest Service makes no warranty, express or implied, nor assumes any liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness, or utility of these geospatial data or for the improper or incorrect use of those data. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible for verifying the limitations of the geospatial data and for using the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
